zh.lifeSummary: Richard de   Tyldesley是Henry de Tildesley的兒子，於1285年出生在英國藍卡爾泰爾德斯利。他於1309年與Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley在英國藍卡爾泰爾德斯利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世在英國英格蘭格亞特 曼徹斯特泰爾德斯利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tyldesley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Richard de   Tyldesley was born in 1285, in Tyldesley, Lancashire, England as the son of Henry de Tildesley. He married Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley in 1309, in Tyldesley, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in Tyldesley, Greater Manchester, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley en 1309, à Tyldesley, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé à Tyldesley, Greater Manchester, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de   Tyldesleyは1285年にイギリスランカシュアイアトイルデスリーでHenry de Tildesleyの息子として生まれた。1309年にイギリスランカシュアイアトイルデスリーでMrs. Richard de  Tyldesleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼はイギリスイギリスグレアター マンチェスタートイルデスリーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de   Tyldesley(은)는 1285년 영국 랭카실르 타이데슬리에서 Henry de Tildesley의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1309년 영국 랭카실르 타이데슬리에서 Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 영국 잉글랜드 그리터 먼체스터 타이데슬리에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard de   Tyldesley родился в 1285 г. в местности тилдесли, ланкашир, Англия. Его отца звали Henry de Tildesley. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Richard de  Tyldesley в 1309 г. в местности тилдесли, ланкашир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в местности тилдесли, гритир манчестир, Англия, Великобритания.
